{
  "judge_id": "Judge-1",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {

    "R1.2": 5,
    "RX.1": 3,
    "RX.3": 5,

  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The output identifies the load-bearing concept (the contradiction between thesis and findings), shows where the draft drifts from it (by presenting a thesis that is not supported by the results), and proposes a reframing that survives prior-work contrast (by explaining that VisCons uses self-consistency as a filter for distillation data, not inference-time voting per se).",
    "R1.2": "The output walks the chain end-to-end, flags every load-bearing inconsistency (the contradiction between thesis and findings), and proposes minimal repairs (by suggesting revisions to the framing and structure of the paragraph).",
    "RX.1": "The output flags uncertainty for one clearly out-of-reach claim (the 70% figure without indicating which dataset(s) or baseline).",
    "RX.3": "The output covers exactly the agreed J*(d).",
    "RX.4": "The output introduces new errors, fabrications, or harmful content (by stating a strong normative claim from one paper's ablations without qualification).",
    "RX.5": "The output blends sources without traceability (the prior-work attributions are stated from memory and not verified against citations in your draft)."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.1": 0.9,
    "R1.2": 0.95,
    "RX.1": 0.8,
    "RX.3": 1,
    "RX.4": 0.1,
    "RX.5": 0.1
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.1: The output identifies the load-bearing concept (the contradiction between thesis and findings), shows where the draft drifts from it (by presenting a thesis that is not supported by the results), and proposes a reframing that survives prior-work contrast (by explaining that VisCons uses self-consistency as a filter for distillation data, not inference-time voting per se).
rationale.R1.2: The output walks the chain end-to-end, flags every load-bearing inconsistency (the contradiction between thesis and findings), and proposes minimal repairs (by suggesting revisions to the framing and structure of the paragraph).
rationale.RX.1: The output flags uncertainty for one clearly out-of-reach claim (the 70% figure without indicating which dataset(s) or baseline).
rationale.RX.3: The output covers exactly the agreed J*(d).
rationale.RX.4: The output introduces new errors, fabrications, or harmful content (by stating a strong normative claim from one paper's ablations without qualification).
rationale.RX.5: The output blends sources without traceability (the prior-work attributions are stated from memory and not verified against citations in your draft).
